mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals expanding supply capacity and economic activity, which historically lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change versus 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above 4.5% signals labor market weakening and impending economic slowdown, reducing corporate profit expectations and producing negative equity returns within a month.
failure_condition: In at least 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are depressed and the federal funds rate is elevated, the joint interaction of tight monetary conditions and weak housing demand amplifies recession fears, causing negative stock returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In a linear regression of 60-day forward returns on HOUST, FEDFUNDS, and their product, the interaction coefficient is not negative and statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment at or below 4.5%), further increases in payroll employment signal overheating that prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, leading to negative equity returns within a month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is 4.5% or below and PAYEMS increases month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth reflects strong consumer spending, directly boosting corporate revenues and short-term equity valuations within a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or insignificant over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1200K signal a meaningful downturn in residential investment, a leading indicator of economic contraction that forecasts negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than half of the periods when HOUST is below 1200.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are elevated alongside high consumer price levels, the combination signals excess demand that provokes monetary tightening, resulting in negative forward equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a linear regression of 20-day forward returns on RSAFS, CPIAUCSL, and their product is not significantly negative (p ≤ 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Once industrial production has already contracted (index below 100), further declines reinforce recession expectations and push equity prices lower over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods when INDPRO is below 100, a decrease in INDPRO is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns more than 50% of the time.
